name: Linden Nj Police & Firemen
description_short: NCUA-insured federal credit union serving Linden, NJ police and firefighters since 1938, offering savings, CDs, mortgages, and loans.
description_long: Linden NJ Police & Firemen Federal Credit Union was established in 1938 and operates as a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union based in Linden, New Jersey. The institution currently holds approximately $7.21 million in total assets with $5.01 million in deposits and $2.16 million in capital, serving a membership base primarily composed of local law enforcement and fire service personnel. The credit union maintains its headquarters at 300 W St George Avenue, PO Box 1520, in Linden, NJ 07036. The credit union offers a comprehensive range of consumer banking products including savings accounts, Certificates of Deposit (CDs), mortgages for both purchase and refinance, home equity lines of credit (HELOC), auto loans, personal loans, and home equity loans. According to their rate information, they provide competitive rates across deposit products, including savings accounts at 4.20% APY, 1-year CDs at 4.59% APY, and 2-year CDs at 4.00% APY. Mortgage offerings include 30-year fixed mortgages at 4.91% APR and 15-year options at 4.39% APR, with HELOC rates starting at 0.99% APR. As a membership-based credit union, Linden NJ Police & Firemen distinguishes itself through its specific membership eligibility tied to law enforcement and fire service employees in the local community. This focused membership model allows the institution to provide products and services tailored to the needs of these professional groups. The credit union maintains NCUA insurance protection for member deposits, providing the same federal safety protections as traditional banks. The institution operates as a smaller regional credit union with limited online rate transparency on their listing page. While they advertise specific rates for various products, the website indicates they are actively working to update rate information. Members seeking current product details are advised to contact the credit union directly, as the platform notes "We're working to get deposit rates for every credit union in the world." The organization appears to serve a niche membership base rather than operating as an open-access institution.
services: Savings accounts with competitive interest rates
Certificates of Deposit (CDs) in multiple term lengths (1-year, 2-year, 5-year)
30-year fixed mortgage loans for home purchases
15-year fixed mortgage loans for accelerated payoff
Personal loans for general borrowing needs
Multiple branch locations in the Linden, NJ area
pros: NCUA-insured institution providing federal deposit protection up to $250,000 per account category
Long operational history since 1938 demonstrates institutional stability and experience
Competitive CD rates at 4.59% for 1-year CDs and 4.00% for 2-year and 5-year terms
Mortgage rates at 4.91% (30-year) and 4.39% (15-year) are competitively positioned
Offers full suite of loan products including auto loans, personal loans, and home equity options
Low HELOC introductory rate of 0.99% APR provides affordable credit access
Focused membership model allows for specialized service to law enforcement and fire service professionals
cons: Restricted membership eligibility limited to police and fire department employees, excluding general consumers
Small asset base of $7.21 million limits product offerings and technology capabilities compared to larger institutions
Website indicates incomplete rate information with note stating rates are not yet available for deposit and loan products
Limited online presence and transparency regarding current product rates and terms as of listing date
No indication of online banking capabilities or digital-first services typical of modern financial institutions
best_for: Linden, NJ police department employees and their families
Local firefighters and fire service personnel seeking member-focused credit union benefits
Law enforcement professionals seeking specialized financial products for their occupational needs
Individuals prioritizing community-based, federally-insured credit union banking over large national banks
diagnosis: Linden NJ Police & Firemen Federal Credit Union is best suited for eligible law enforcement and fire service employees in the Linden area seeking federally-insured savings, lending, and mortgage products with a community-focused institution. The primary limitation is restricted membership eligibility—this credit union cannot serve the general public and requires employment verification with qualifying agencies.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
